title: Apple Silicon VRAM gauge 0/0 + status-bar progress + generation duration (3 fixes)
description: On macOS Apple Silicon the status-bar VRAM gauge shows '0.0 / 0 GB' because Apple's unified memory has no discrete VRAM device to probe — the GPU shares system RAM. Observed on the M4 rentamac test (2026-06-10, 0.0.9). Cosmetic but looks broken to users. Fix options: (a) on Apple Silicon, hide the VRAM gauge entirely and show only the RAM gauge (which already reads correctly, e.g. 6.2/16 GB); or (b) relabel/repurpose the VRAM gauge as 'unified/shared' on Apple Silicon and feed it the MPS allocated figure (torch.mps.current_allocated_memory) if cheaply available. Where: the status-bar VRAM/RAM gauge component + whatever backend telemetry feeds vram (grep for the VRAM gauge + the system telemetry route). Gate the behavior on the already-detected vendor === 'apple'. NOT a 1.0.0 blocker — cosmetic polish.
